description:
You are an incident responder who has been asked to migrate a single table from an old forensic SQLite database to a fresh one and to prove, in writing, that the migration succeeded.

Environment already present when you begin:
1. A source database at /home/user/source.db
   • It contains one table called network_logs with exactly five (5) rows.
2. No destination database yet exists.

Your objectives:
A. Copy (migrate) the complete network_logs table—including its schema and every row—from /home/user/source.db into a brand-new database named /home/user/destination.db.  
B. Count the number of rows in network_logs in BOTH databases after the copy.  
C. Produce a human-readable validation report at /home/user/migration_validation.log whose contents are **exactly** (no leading/trailing spaces, blank lines, or additional text):

MIGRATION VALIDATION REPORT  
Source row count: 5  
Destination row count: 5  
Status: SUCCESS

Important notes for the report:
• Each line must end with a single newline (UNIX line ending, “\n”).  
• The field names (“Source row count” etc.) are case-sensitive.  
• Do not include paths or any extra commentary in the log. Only the four lines shown above.

Operational constraints:
• Accomplish all three objectives using a total of two or three terminal commands (the incident-response team wants a concise command history).  
• You do NOT need super-user privileges.  
• The sqlite3 CLI is available system-wide.  
• If you create any temporary files, remove them when finished.  
• When you are done, you should be able to run
  sqlite3 /home/user/destination.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM network_logs;"
  and see “5”.

The automated grader will check:
1. That /home/user/destination.db exists and contains a table called network_logs.  
2. That the destination table’s schema matches the source.  
3. That both tables hold exactly five rows each.  
4. That /home/user/migration_validation.log exists and matches the four-line format shown above **character for character**.

If any step fails, the task is considered incomplete. Proceed to the terminal and complete the migration and validation.
truth:
Initial file that exists when the task container starts:
Path: /home/user/source.db

Sqlite3 dump of /home/user/source.db (for reference):

PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF;
BEGIN TRANSACTION;
CREATE TABLE network_logs(
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  timestamp TEXT,
  src_ip TEXT,
  dest_ip TEXT,
  action TEXT
);
INSERT INTO network_logs VALUES(1,'2023-06-01T12:00:00Z','10.0.0.5','192.168.1.20','ALLOW');
INSERT INTO network_logs VALUES(2,'2023-06-01T12:05:00Z','10.0.0.5','172.16.0.8','ALLOW');
INSERT INTO network_logs VALUES(3,'2023-06-01T12:10:00Z','10.0.0.10','192.168.1.21','DENY');
INSERT INTO network_logs VALUES(4,'2023-06-01T12:15:00Z','10.0.0.15','192.168.1.22','ALLOW');
INSERT INTO network_logs VALUES(5,'2023-06-01T12:20:00Z','10.0.0.20','192.168.1.23','DENY');
COMMIT;

Files/directories expected after successful agent execution:

1. /home/user/destination.db
   • Must contain a table network_logs with identical schema and data as above.
   • Query “SELECT COUNT(*) FROM network_logs;” must return 5.

2. /home/user/migration_validation.log
Exact desired content (with \n line endings):
MIGRATION VALIDATION REPORT
Source row count: 5
Destination row count: 5
Status: SUCCESS

No other files are required. The agent should not change /home/user/source.db. The agent may create and delete temporary files within /home/user, but they will not be checked.

Automated grading will:
• Verify destination.db exists.  
• Check that `.schema network_logs` matches the source schema exactly.  
• Ensure both tables contain the same five rows.  
• Read migration_validation.log and compare byte-for-byte with the expected four-line content above.
name: task_000000_cb32c375
